name: serializable

metadata.name: serializable
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Base class with serialization helpers for user-defined Python objects

documentation: [![Tests](https://github.com/openvax/serializable/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openvax/serializable/actions/workflows/tests.yml) [![PyPI downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/serializable.svg)](https://pypistats.org/packages/serializable) # serializable Base class with serialization methods for user-defined Python objects ## Usage Classes which inherit from `Serializable` are enabled with default implementations of `to_json`, `from_json`, `__reduce__` (for pickling), and other serialization helpers. A derived class must either: - have a member data matching the name of each argument to `__init__` - provide a user-defined `to_dict()` method which returns a dictionary whose keys match the arguments to `__init__` If you change the keyword arguments to a class which derives from `Serializable` but would like to be able to deserialize older JSON representations then you can define a class-level dictionary called `_KEYWORD_ALIASES` which maps old keywords to new names (or `None` if a keyword was removed). ## Limitations - Serializable objects must inherit from `Serializable`, be tuples or namedtuples, be serializble primitive types such as dict, list, int, float, or str. - The serialized representation of objects relies on reserved keywords (such as `"__name__"`, and `"__class__"`), so dictionaries are expected to not contain any keys which begin with two underscores.

modules.name: serializable.primitive_types
documentation:
A type is considered "primitive" if it is a non-collection type which can
be serialized and deserialized successfully.

functions.name: serializable.primitive_types.return_primitive
documentation:
Decorator which wraps a single argument function to ignore any
arguments of primitive type (simply returning them unmodified).

classes.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable.to_dict
documentation:
Returns a dictionary which can be used to reconstruct an instance
of a derived class (typically by matching args to __init__). The values
of the returned dictionary must be primitive atomic types
(bool, string, int, float), primitive collections
(int, list, tuple, set) or instances of Serializable.

The default implementation is to assume all the arguments to __init__
have fields of the same name on a serializable object.

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable._reconstruct_nested_objects
documentation:
Nested serializable objects will be represented as dictionaries so we
allow manual reconstruction of those objects in this method.

By default just returns the state dictionary unmodified.

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable._update_kwargs
documentation: Rename any old keyword arguments to preserve backwards compatibility

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable.from_dict
documentation:
Given a dictionary of flattened fields (result of calling to_dict()),
returns an instance.

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable.to_json
documentation: Returns a string containing a JSON representation of this Genome.

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable.from_json
documentation: Reconstruct an instance from a JSON string.

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable.write_json_file
documentation:
Serialize this VariantCollection to a JSON representation and write it
out to a text file.

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable.read_json_file
documentation: Construct a VariantCollection from a JSON file.

methods.name: serializable.serializable.Serializable.__reduce__
documentation:
Overriding this method directs the default pickler to reconstruct
this object using our from_dict method.

Base class for all PyEnsembl objects which provides default
methods such as to_json, from_json, __reduce__, and from_dict

Relies on the following condition:
     (1) a user-defined to_dict method
     (2) the keys of to_dict() must match the arguments to __init__

modules.name: serializable.helpers
documentation:
Helper functions for deconstructing classes, functions, and user-defined
objects into serializable types.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.init_arg_names
documentation: Names of arguments to __init__ method of this object's class.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.class_from_serializable_representation
documentation:
Given the name of a module and a class it contains, imports that module
and gets the class object from it.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.class_to_serializable_representation
documentation:
Given a class, return two strings:
    - fully qualified import path for its module
    - name of the class

The class can be reconstructed from these two strings by calling
class_from_serializable_representation.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.function_from_serializable_representation
documentation:
Given the name of a module and a function it contains, imports that module
and gets the class object from it.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.function_to_serializable_representation
documentation:
Converts a Python function into a serializable representation. Does not
currently work for methods or functions with closure data.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.parse_serialized_keys_index
documentation:
Given a field name such as __serialized_keys__element_10 returns the integer 10
but returns None for other strings.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.dict_to_serializable_repr
documentation:
Recursively convert values of dictionary to serializable representations.
Convert non-string keys to JSON representations and replace them in the
dictionary with indices of unique JSON strings (e.g. __1, __2, etc..).

functions.name: serializable.helpers.from_serializable_dict
documentation:
Reconstruct a dictionary by recursively reconstructing all its keys and
values.

This is the most hackish part since we rely on key names such as
__name__, __class__, __module__ as metadata about how to reconstruct
an object.

TODO:
    It would be cleaner to always wrap each object in a layer of type
    metadata and then have an inner dictionary which represents the
    flattened result of to_dict() for user-defined objects.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.to_dict
documentation:
If value wasn't isn't a primitive scalar or collection then it needs to
either implement to_dict (instances of Serializable) or has member
data matching each required arg of __init__.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.to_serializable_repr
documentation:
Convert an instance of Serializable or a primitive collection containing
such instances into serializable types.

functions.name: serializable.helpers.to_json
documentation:
Returns JSON representation of a given Serializable instance or
other primitive object.
